title: Boundary cases (m>k, m=1) and global telescope consistency

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Verify degenerate behaviour and the global check:
(i) m>k: RHS is the empty sum (=0), and R^{k,l,m}=R^{k,l} since e_{k+1}=...=e_l=0 in R^{k,l}; so the quotient is 0. Confirm the formula reproduces this.
(ii) m=1: subalgebra Q[e_1]; formula gives q*[l]_q * sum_{j=0}^{k-1} q^{j l}. Verify directly.
(iii) Global telescope: sum_{m=1}^{l} RHS(k,l,m) = qbinom(k+l,k) - 1 = Hilb(R^{k,l}) minus the degree-0 part. A nontrivial q-identity and a strong correctness check (verified numerically); a complete proof should derive it as a corollary.

Mechanical once the basis lemma and q-identity are in place, but omitting m>k consistency and the telescope leaves the proof incomplete.
